content: UIEB is an underwater image enhancement benchmark with 950 real-world underwater images, including 890 images with reference images and 60 challenging images. It is suitable for color-cast, low-contrast and enhancement evaluation research. The official project restricts the data to non-commercial research and prohibits redistribution.

Aquarium Data COTS is a compact object-detection dataset with 638 images and seven classes: fish, jellyfish, penguin, puffin, shark, starfish and stingray. It is convenient for a small YOLO demonstration, but aquarium scenes are not identical to natural seafloor scenes.

DUO means Detecting Underwater Objects. It targets underwater robot-picking scenes and commonly uses four biological classes: holothurian, echinus, scallop and starfish. It is a better domain benchmark for seafloor biological detection than aquarium imagery.
